title: Add option to "podman generate systemd" to generate template unit files (name@.service)
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**


/kind feature

**Description**

Add a command-line option to `podman generate systemd` for generating [template unit files](https://fedoramagazine.org/systemd-template-unit-files/).
The generated unit files would make use of `%i`and `%I` to differentiate between the different instances of the unit.

Creating an example of how such a template unit file could look like:

```
[esjolund@laptop ~]$ podman run --name foobar -d docker.io/library/nginx:latest
Trying to pull docker.io/library/nginx:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob aa1efa14b3bf done  
Copying blob cf16cd8e71e0 done  
Copying blob b78b95af9b17 done  
Copying blob 0241c68333ef done  
Copying blob f7ec5a41d630 done  
Copying blob c7d6bca2b8dc done  
Copying config 62d49f9bab done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
4c6c20e7d2e004b0e6945ad5dce645cb7684d8a734d358959b13e31b3d0ec48f
[esjolund@laptop ~]$ podman generate systemd --new -n foobar > foobar.service
[esjolund@laptop ~]$ cp foobar.service foobar@.service
[esjolund@laptop ~]$ emacs foobar@.service
```
The file _foobar@.service_ could for instance look like this:
```
# container-foobar@.service
# autogenerated by Podman 3.1.2
# Sat May  8 10:08:37 CEST 2021

[Unit]
Description=Podman container-foobar service for %I
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=/home/erikdev/.local/share/containers/storage /run/user/1002/containers

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStartPre=/bin/rm -f %t/container-foobar-%i.pid %t/container-foobar-%i.ctr-id
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --conmon-pidfile %t/container-foobar-%i.pid --cidfile %t/container-foobar-%i.ctr-id --cgroups=no-conmon --replace --name foobar -d docker.io/library/nginx:latest
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop --ignore --cidfile %t/container-foobar-%i.ctr-id -t 10
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm --ignore -f --cidfile %t/container-foobar-%i.ctr-id
PIDFile=%t/container-foobar-%i.pid
Type=forking

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```



``` diff
--- foobar.service	2021-05-08 10:08:37.794065962 +0200
+++ foobar@.service	2021-05-08 10:18:14.825403306 +0200
@@ -1,9 +1,9 @@
-# container-foobar.service
+# container-foobar@.service
 # autogenerated by Podman 3.1.2
 # Sat May  8 10:08:37 CEST 2021
 
 [Unit]
-Description=Podman container-foobar.service
+Description=Podman container-foobar service for %I
 Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
 Wants=network.target
 After=network-online.target
@@ -13,11 +13,11 @@
 Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
 Restart=on-failure
 TimeoutStopSec=70
-ExecStartPre=/bin/rm -f %t/container-foobar.pid %t/container-foobar.ctr-id
-ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --conmon-pidfile %t/container-foobar.pid --cidfile %t/container-foobar.ctr-id --cgroups=no-conmon --replace --name foobar -d docker.io/library/nginx:latest
-ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop --ignore --cidfile %t/container-foobar.ctr-id -t 10
-ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm --ignore -f --cidfile %t/container-foobar.ctr-id
-PIDFile=%t/container-foobar.pid
+ExecStartPre=/bin/rm -f %t/container-foobar-%i.pid %t/container-foobar-%i.ctr-id
+ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --conmon-pidfile %t/container-foobar-%i.pid --cidfile %t/container-foobar-%i.ctr-id --cgroups=no-conmon --replace --name foobar -d docker.io/library/nginx:latest
+ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop --ignore --cidfile %t/container-foobar-%i.ctr-id -t 10
+ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm --ignore -f --cidfile %t/container-foobar-%i.ctr-id
+PIDFile=%t/container-foobar-%i.pid
 Type=forking
 
 [Install]
```

comments.body:
Thanks for opening the issue.

Can you pitch the idea a bit more? What's the benefit of templates over the current unit files?

@vrothberg I think there is a use for both the standard unit files and the template unit files and I would like `podman generate systemd` to be able to generate both. 

I believe that template unit files are used whenever you need to have multiple instances of the same service. A quick look under _/usr/lib/systemd/system_ gives a few examples

```
[esjolund@laptop ~]$ cat /etc/fedora-release 
Fedora release 34 (Thirty Four)
[esjolund@laptop ~]$ for i in $(grep -r --include  '*@.service'  %i /usr/lib/systemd/system/ -l); do if ! grep -q Type=oneshot $i ; then  grep -H ExecStart= $i |grep %i; fi; done
/usr/lib/systemd/system/configure-printer@.service:ExecStart=/usr/lib/udev/udev-configure-printer add "%i"
/usr/lib/systemd/system/httpd@.service:ExecStart=/usr/sbin/httpd $OPTIONS -DFOREGROUND -f conf/%i.conf
/usr/lib/systemd/system/openvpn-client@.service:ExecStart=/usr/sbin/openvpn --suppress-timestamps --nobind --config %i.conf
/usr/lib/systemd/system/openvpn-server@.service:ExecStart=/usr/sbin/openvpn --status %t/openvpn-server/status-%i.log --status-version 2 --suppress-timestamps --cipher AES-256-GCM --data-ciphers AES-256-GCM:AES-128-GCM:AES-256-CBC:AES-128-CBC:BF-CBC --config %i.conf
/usr/lib/systemd/system/systemd-journald@.service:ExecStart=/usr/lib/systemd/systemd-journald %i
/usr/lib/systemd/system/systemd-nspawn@.service:ExecStart=systemd-nspawn --quiet --keep-unit --boot --link-journal=try-guest --network-veth -U --settings=override --machine=%i
/usr/lib/systemd/system/teamd@.service:ExecStart=/usr/bin/teamd -U -D -o -t %i -f /run/teamd/%i.conf
[esjolund@laptop ~]$ 
```

Note that dependencies can also be specified with "%i" as can be seen here

```
[esjolund@laptop ~]$ for i in $(grep -r --include  '*@.service'  %i /usr/lib/systemd/system/ -l); do cat $i ; done|grep After | grep %i
After=dev-%i.device systemd-user-sessions.service plymouth-quit-wait.service getty-pre.target
After=systemd-journald@%i.socket systemd-journald-varlink@%i.socket
After=%i.device systemd-fsck-root.service local-fs-pre.target
After=%i.device
After=systemd-user-sessions.service user-runtime-dir@%i.service dbus.service
After=dev-%i.device
[esjolund@laptop ~]$ for i in $(grep -r --include  '*@.service'  %i /usr/lib/systemd/system/ -l); do cat $i ; done|grep Requires | grep %i
Requires=systemd-journald@%i.socket systemd-journald-varlink@%i.socket
RequiresMountsFor=/var/lib/machines/%i
Requires=user-runtime-dir@%i.service
[esjolund@laptop ~]$ 
```

Starting a systemd service from a template like this
```
systemctl start foobar@firstinstance.service
```
could therefore start other "templated" services at the same time.

I could probably improve the pitch but this was a start.

One thing that I forgot in the original request:
The value of `PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT` would need to be unique too. So this
```
 Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
```
would need to be changed to something like
```
 Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n-%i
```
Thanks, @eriksjolund!  Do you envision templates for containers only or also for pods?
@vrothberg It should be a need for pods too.
This SGTM
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@eriksjolund interested in opening a PR for this?

@rhatdan No, not at the moment. (Maybe in the future but there are other things that attract my interest more for the moment being)

__Update__ (30 July 2021): I strikethrough the discussion below about the user interface. I haven't really figured out what user interface is the best.
~Regarding the user interface, I think this command-line option only makes sense combined with `--new`.~

~There seems to be two alternatives:~

~__Alternative 1:__ Trigger a failure and provide an informative error message whenever `--new` is missing.~
~__Alternative 2:__ Let the usage of this command-line option also imply that `--new` is set~

~I see that both ways have their merits, but I slightly prefer __alternative 1__.~

@rhatdan @vrothberg mind assigning this to me?

![](https://media.giphy.com/media/xT1XGOGdyDrL2BTfxK/giphy.gif)
It's all yours, @boaz0! Thank you
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@boaz0 Any progress?
Sorry, I was sick in the last 2 weeks. I will be back to it in the following days.
Hope you'll feel better soon, @boaz0
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
still working on it. I am now testing it on my machine looking for edge cases or issues.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

waiting for code review... :crocodile:

I am reviewing daily but my comments must be addressed.
